mission.title: open-policy-agent-opa-: Support of OAuth 2.0 client credentials authentication for service API's
mission.description: Support of OAuth 2.0 client credentials authentication for service API's. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-policy-agent-opa- deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-policy-agent-opa- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
helm list -n open-policy-agent-opa- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-policy-agent-opa- installation.

steps.title: Review open-policy-agent-opa- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-policy-agent-opa- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get configmap -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-policy-agent-opa-
```
Having support for OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow for OPA it can automatically get access/refresh tokens from authori

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support of OAuth 2.0 client credentials authentication for
steps.description:
WORK IN PROGRESS

Early feedback welcomed.

This PR is a potential approach for https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa/issues/2057   

Summary:
 * Add in a new command `io.openid.verify` to verify and parse on successful verification an id token.
 * go-oidc is a new dependency since OpenID Connect is a standard and well qualified implementations exist
 * Aim here is simple interface, hide the ugliness, do the right thing under the hood, and use the standard to make life simpler for

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get events -n open-policy-agent-opa- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support of OAuth 2.0 client credentials authentication for" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
WORK IN PROGRESS

Early feedback welcomed.

This PR is a potential approach for https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa/issues/2057   

Summary:
 * Add in a new command `io.openid.verify` to verify and parse on successful verification an id token.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-policy-agent-opa- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
